This article about an Italian film director is a [stub](/wiki/Wikipedia:Stub "Wikipedia:Stub"). You can help Wikipedia by [adding missing information](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Guido_Brignone&action=edit).

[newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Guido+Brignone%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Guido+Brignone%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Guido+Brignone%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Guido+Brignone%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(June 2019)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

(6 December 1886 – 6 March 1959) was an Italian film director and actor.[[1]](#cite_note-Moliterno2008-1) He was the father of actress [Lilla Brignone](/wiki/Lilla_Brignone "Lilla Brignone") and younger brother of actress [Mercedes Brignone](/wiki/Mercedes_Brignone "Mercedes Brignone").

Gino Moliterno (29 September 2008). [*Historical Dictionary of Italian Cinema*](https://books.google.com/books?id=l0dUgcYI8B0C&pg=PA347). Scarecrow Press. p. 53. [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [978-0-8108-6254-8](/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-0-8108-6254-8 "Special:BookSources/978-0-8108-6254-8").

Workman, Christopher; Howarth, Troy (2016). "Tome of Terror: Horror Films of the Silent Era". Midnight Marquee Press. p. 288. [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [978-1936168-68-2](/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-1936168-68-2 "Special:BookSources/978-1936168-68-2").

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Italian film director and actor

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [icon](/wiki/File:Question_book-new.svg) | This article **needs additional citations for [verification](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability "Wikipedia:Verifiability")**. Please help [improve this article](/wiki/Special:EditPage/Guido_Brignone "Special:EditPage/Guido Brignone") by [adding citations to reliable sources](/wiki/Help:Referencing_for_beginners "Help:Referencing for beginners"). Unsourced material may be challenged and removed. *Find sources:* ["Guido Brignone"](https://www.google.com/search?as_eq=wikipedia&q=%22Guido+Brignone%22) – [news](https://www.google.com/search?tbm=nws&q=%22Guido+Brignone%22+-wikipedia&tbs=ar:1) **·** [newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Guido+Brignone%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Guido+Brignone%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Guido+Brignone%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Guido+Brignone%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(June 2019)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

| Guido Brignone | |
| --- | --- |
| Guido Brignone in 1930 | |
| Born | (1886-12-06)6 December 1886  [Milan](/wiki/Milan "Milan"), [Italy](/wiki/Kingdom_of_Italy "Kingdom of Italy") |
| Died | 6 March 1959(1959-03-06) (aged 72)  [Rome](/wiki/Rome "Rome"), Italy |
| Occupation | [Film director](/wiki/Film_director "Film director") |
| Years active | 1925–1959 |

**Guido Brignone** (6 December 1886 – 6 March 1959) was an Italian film director and actor.[[1]](#cite_note-Moliterno2008-1) He was the father of actress [Lilla Brignone](/wiki/Lilla_Brignone "Lilla Brignone") and younger brother of actress [Mercedes Brignone](/wiki/Mercedes_Brignone "Mercedes Brignone").

Brignone was born in [Milan](/wiki/Milan "Milan"), [Italy](/wiki/Italy "Italy"). He was the first Italian Director to win the [Venice Film Festival](/wiki/Venice_Film_Festival "Venice Film Festival") or Mostra Internazionale d'Arte Cinematografica, the oldest film festival in the world, with Best Italian Film, *[Teresa Confalonieri](/wiki/Loyalty_of_Love "Loyalty of Love")* (1934).

He died in [Rome](/wiki/Rome "Rome") in 1959.
